summary: metemask-sdk typosquat exfiltrates host and filesystem data via postinstall
details: The npm package @wagni_bot/metemask-sdk is a typosquat of the widely used MetaMask SDK naming ("metemask" vs "metamask"). Its postinstall.js executes automatically on `npm install` and combines host reconnaissance with filesystem enumeration and outbound HTTP: it imports `fs`, `os`, and `http`; reads host identifiers via `os.hostname()` and `os.userInfo()`; walks the user's home directory via `os.homedir()` with repeated `fs.existsSync` probes; and sends the collected data over HTTP. This matches the wallet/credential exfiltration pattern where postinstall probes home-directory paths (typically wallet, keystore, SSH, npmrc, and shell-config locations) and ships the results to an attacker-controlled endpoint. Installing this package causes credential-grade data disclosure from the installer's machine at install time, with no legitimate SDK functionality to justify the behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
